description.en: Format, beautify, and validate JSON in your browser for free. Paste messy JSON and get clean, indented output instantly — no server required.

en: Paste your JSON text into the editor or upload a .json file
Click Beautify to format and indent the JSON automatically
Review any validation errors highlighted in the editor
Copy the formatted JSON or download it as a file

description.en: Format JSON online for free. Paste any JSON string and get beautifully indented output instantly — no software install required.

question.en: Is there a free online JSON formatter?

answer.en: Yes. Our JSON beautifier is free, requires no account, and runs entirely in your browser. Paste any JSON and get formatted output instantly.

question.en: Is it safe to paste sensitive JSON data into an online formatter?

answer.en: Yes. Our formatter runs entirely in your browser — no data is sent to any server. You can safely paste API responses, config files, and sensitive JSON.

description.en: Prettify JSON by adding indentation and line breaks to minified or compact JSON. Makes JSON human-readable in one click.

question.en: What does prettify JSON mean?

answer.en: Prettifying JSON means converting compact, single-line JSON into an indented, multi-line format that is easy to read. Our tool adds consistent indentation and line breaks automatically.

question.en: How do I prettify minified JSON?

answer.en: Paste your minified JSON into our beautifier and click Beautify. The tool will expand the JSON with proper indentation — typically 2 or 4 spaces per level.

description.en: Validate and format JSON in one step. Our formatter highlights syntax errors and shows exactly where your JSON is invalid.

question.en: How do I validate and format JSON at the same time?

answer.en: Paste your JSON into our tool and click Beautify. Invalid JSON will be flagged with a clear error message showing the line and character position of the problem.

question.en: What are common JSON validation errors?

answer.en: Common errors include trailing commas (not allowed in JSON), single quotes instead of double quotes, unescaped special characters, and missing or extra brackets and braces.

description.en: Minify JSON by removing whitespace and line breaks. Produce compact JSON for API payloads, configuration files, and production code.

question.en: How do I minify JSON online?

answer.en: Paste your formatted JSON into our tool and click Minify. All whitespace, indentation, and line breaks are removed to produce the smallest possible valid JSON string.

question.en: Why would I want to minify JSON?

answer.en: Minified JSON is smaller, which reduces data transfer sizes in APIs and web applications. It is the standard for production API payloads and embedded JSON configuration.

question.en: Is the JSON formatter free?

answer.en: Yes, completely free. No account, no sign-up, no limits. Works in any browser.

question.en: Does the JSON formatter work offline?

answer.en: Yes. After the page loads, all JSON formatting happens in your browser. You can disconnect from the internet and continue using the tool.

question.en: What is the difference between JSON beautify and minify?

answer.en: Beautify adds indentation and line breaks to make JSON human-readable. Minify removes all whitespace to make JSON as compact as possible for machine processing.

description.en: Encode text or files to Base64 and decode Base64 strings back to plain text or binary — all free, all in your browser.

en: Choose Encode or Decode mode using the toggle
Paste your text, or upload a file to encode
Click Encode or Decode to process instantly
Copy the result or download the output file

description.en: Encode and decode Base64 online for free. No software installation — works in any modern browser with no data sent to a server.

question.en: Is there a free online Base64 encoder and decoder?

answer.en: Yes. Our Base64 encoder/decoder is free, requires no account, and runs entirely in your browser. Both encoding and decoding are instantaneous.

question.en: Is it safe to encode sensitive data with an online Base64 tool?

answer.en: Yes. Our tool processes everything locally in your browser. No data is sent to any server, so it is safe to encode passwords, tokens, and confidential strings.

description.en: Encode plain text to Base64 or decode a Base64 string back to plain text. Handles UTF-8 text including Unicode characters.

question.en: How do I encode a text string to Base64?

answer.en: Select 'Encode' mode, paste your text into the input box, and click Encode. The Base64 output appears instantly in the result field.

question.en: Does the Base64 encoder handle Unicode and special characters?

answer.en: Yes. Our encoder handles full UTF-8 text including accented characters, emoji, and non-Latin scripts. The encoded output is valid Base64 that can be decoded back to the original text.

description.en: Convert an image file to a Base64 data URL. Useful for embedding images directly in HTML, CSS, or JSON without external file references.

question.en: How do I convert an image to Base64?

answer.en: Upload your image file using the file picker. Our tool reads the image and outputs a Base64-encoded data URL (e.g., data:image/png;base64,...) that you can paste directly into HTML or CSS.

question.en: Why would I convert an image to Base64?

answer.en: Base64 images can be embedded inline in HTML, CSS, or JSON without needing a separate image file or HTTP request. This is useful for small icons, email templates, and data URIs.

description.en: Decode Base64 strings back to plain text or binary files online. Paste any Base64 string and get the original content instantly in your browser.

question.en: How do I decode a Base64 string online?

answer.en: Select 'Decode' mode, paste your Base64 string into the input, and click Decode. If the original data was text, it appears in the output. If it was a file, you can download it.

question.en: What can I decode from Base64?

answer.en: Base64 can encode any binary data — plain text, images, PDFs, or any file type. Our decoder handles all of these and lets you download the decoded binary output.

question.en: What is Base64?

answer.en: Base64 is an encoding scheme that converts binary data into a set of 64 printable ASCII characters. It is commonly used to embed binary data in text-based formats like JSON, HTML, CSS, and email.

question.en: Is Base64 encoding the same as encryption?

answer.en: No. Base64 is encoding, not encryption. Anyone can decode a Base64 string — it provides no security. Use it only for format compatibility, not to protect sensitive data.

description.en: Convert CSV files to JSON format for free in your browser. Paste CSV data or upload a file and get valid JSON output instantly — no server required.

en: Paste your CSV text or upload a .csv file
Confirm that column headers are detected correctly
Click Convert to generate JSON in your browser
Copy the JSON output or download it as a .json file

description.en: Convert CSV to JSON online for free. No account, no upload, no limits — paste your CSV and get JSON output instantly in your browser.

question.en: Is there a free tool to convert CSV to JSON online?

answer.en: Yes. Our CSV to JSON converter is free and requires no account. Paste your CSV data and get formatted JSON output immediately — all in your browser.

question.en: Is it safe to use an online tool to convert CSV to JSON?

answer.en: Yes. Our tool processes data entirely in your browser. No CSV data is sent to any server, so it is safe for sensitive spreadsheet data.

description.en: Convert CSV with headers to JSON objects. The first row of your CSV becomes the keys in each JSON object — perfect for structured data.

question.en: How do CSV headers map to JSON when converting?

answer.en: The first row of your CSV is treated as column headers. Each subsequent row becomes a JSON object where the header names are the keys and the row values are the values.

question.en: What if my CSV does not have headers?

answer.en: Disable the 'First row is header' option. Our converter will then use column indexes (column_0, column_1, etc.) as JSON keys, or output an array of arrays.

description.en: Convert Excel spreadsheet data to JSON by exporting to CSV first. Our tool handles CSV files exported from Excel, Google Sheets, and LibreOffice Calc.

question.en: How do I convert an Excel file to JSON?

answer.en: In Excel, go to File > Save As and choose CSV format. Then upload the CSV file to our converter to get JSON output. This works with Excel, Google Sheets, and LibreOffice Calc.

question.en: Does the CSV to JSON converter handle Excel-style CSV with commas in quoted fields?

answer.en: Yes. Our converter correctly parses RFC 4180 CSV, including fields enclosed in double quotes that contain commas, newlines, or other special characters.

question.en: Is the CSV to JSON converter free?

answer.en: Yes, completely free. No account, no file size limits, no watermarks. All conversion runs in your browser.

question.en: What JSON structure does the CSV produce?

answer.en: By default, each CSV row becomes a JSON object with header names as keys. The result is an array of objects — the most common format for working with tabular data in JavaScript and APIs.
